track_prompt: Synthesize editorial-02 and generative-02 into a polished reusable Loop Lab SVG event-card system. Preserve editorial hierarchy, typography, grid discipline, accessibility, and real variant tokens; preserve generative modular loop/signal-path distinctiveness. Repair the independently detected CTA contrast and title/panel intrusion defects. Produce standalone card.svg, a three-variant system sheet, tokens, objective metrics, and explicit evidence-led synthesis notes.
input_feedback: The parent panel split. Opus and Terra ranked editorial-02 first because hierarchy, information clarity, contrast, and variant-ready tokens were more complete; Gemini preferred generative-02 because the modular routing panel was the most memorable and brand-appropriate image. All agreed the generative language was distinctive. Opus independently found two metric defects hidden by the generative self-review: white on #3B82F6 is 3.68:1, not 4.5:1, and the title may extend about 24px into the panel. Editorial-02 also retained fixed badge-width and long-content boundary risks.
judge_feedback: Provisional synthesis observation after real Inkscape rendering: Loop 01 combines an editorial title/metadata/CTA reading order with a protected right routing module. Both parent defects are repaired: CTA contrast is 11.67:1 and the rendered title ends at x=691.262 before the panel at x=760, a measured 68.738px safe zone. However, variants-preview.png reveals a new concrete defect: compact variant 02's venue 'HUB A + WEB' escapes the card. Inkscape measures the text bbox right edge at x=779.358, 3.358px beyond the card's outer edge x=776. The no-clipping/layout-containment gate therefore fails. The compact fixed three-column model is not resilient even at its published 11-character venue budget.
next_prompt: Keep Loop 01's protected zones, contrast-safe CTA, and signal-path language. Materially repair the observed compact venue overset with width-aware metadata cells that wrap/stack flexible content. Also render a stress-test display event with a three-line title, long venue, and longer delivery label; add explicit title-density tiers and content-driven badge width rules; remeasure every major bbox, contrast pair, and safe zone without reducing hierarchy.
